gene_region.label: DPYD transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical DPYD genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: DPYD encodes dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase, the rate-limiting enzyme in fluoropyrimidine catabolism. This bundle emphasizes pyrimidine catabolism, 5-fluorouracil/capecitabine clearance, toxicity susceptibility, and pharmacogenomic dosing biology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local DPYD bundle is fluoropyrimidine pharmacogenomics oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: DPYD variants can alter or tag pyrimidine catabolism, 5-fluorouracil/capecitabine clearance, toxicity susceptibility, and pharmacogenomic dosing biology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: DPYD methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a pyrimidine catabolism, 5-fluorouracil/capecitabine clearance, toxicity susceptibility, and pharmacogenomic dosing biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for DPYD.
Because DPYD biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against pyrimidine catabolism, 5-fluorouracil/capecitabine clearance, toxicity susceptibility, and pharmacogenomic dosing biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use DPYD promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for pyrimidine catabolism, 5-fluorouracil/capecitabine clearance, toxicity susceptibility, and pharmacogenomic dosing biology.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000188641: DPYD coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: DPYD biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: DPYD: pyrimidine catabolism, 5-fluorouracil/capecitabine clearance, toxicity susceptibility, and pharmacogenomic dosing biology
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a DPYD fluoropyrimidine pharmacogenomics thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to pyrimidine catabolism, 5-fluorouracil/capecitabine clearance, toxicity susceptibility, and pharmacogenomic dosing biology and DPYD fluoropyrimidine toxicity context, Pyrimidine catabolism. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs3918290 (DPYD*2A) is curated as a DPYD marker for DPYD fluoropyrimidine toxicity context, Pyrimidine catabolism. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the pyrimidine catabolism, 5-fluorouracil/capecitabine clearance, toxicity susceptibility, and pharmacogenomic dosing biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag pyrimidine catabolism, 5-fluorouracil/capecitabine clearance, toxicity susceptibility, and pharmacogenomic dosing biology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
research_context: Use this marker as a DPYD pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs3918290 (DPYD*2A) with DPYD methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
